description: This set of bright-field transmission electron microscopy (TEM) micrographs shows the dislocation structure of room-temperature 2~3% compressed Fe-28at.%Al alloy, which corresponds to the composition with peak work hardening rate in the Fe-Al alloy system. The scale bar is 0.5μm, the zone axis is near [0 0 1], and the imaging vector marked by the arrow is 2 0 0. Abundant superdislocations are observed, most of which are straight, in near-screw orientations, and lie on {1 1 0} glide planes with different Burgers vectors. These superdislocations are perfect superdislocations for B2 lattice composed of two 1/2<111> partials, trailing APBII faults during gliding. The uniform distribution of these near-screw superdislocations, together with subsequently formed dislocation dipoles and sessile <100> edge dislocations from superdislocation reactions, contributes to the sharp work hardening peak in 26~30at.% Al Fe-Al alloys.
